verdict.label: RED · Stand down / roll DTE longer
verdict.desc: This is the single loudest event day of the summer: June CPI drops at 8:30 AM ET and Fed Chair Warsh testifies to Congress the same morning, on top of a live US–Iran war. Overnight the U.S. hit Iran a third straight night and moves to enforce a Strait of Hormuz blockade at 4 PM ET today with a 20% cargo toll — oil has jumped double-digits on the week (Brent ~$85–87, WTI ~$80), the VIX popped to ~17, and bond traders are now betting on a July Fed hike. Two independent GREEN lockouts are live: a major inflation print inside 24h AND composite back in WARM. Stand down on new credit spreads today, or push DTE well past both the data and the conflict window. Cash is a position.

composite.band_note: WARM · up from 66 · CPI-day + Hormuz blockade

gap.heading: P(≥1% Overnight Gap to Wed Open)
gap.band_note: ABOVE the 35% danger line · GREEN locked out

focus.jbig: CPI at 8:30 · Warsh testifies · Hormuz toll · stand down
focus.jtag: June CPI + Fed testimony land the same morning US enforces a Hormuz blockade — oil spikes, banks report
focus.jnote: Everything lands at once today. June CPI prints at 8:30 AM ET — consensus is a soft headline (~−0.1% m/m, ~3.8–3.9% y/y) on a 10% June gasoline drop, but core is still sticky near 2.9%, and bond traders are lifting bets on a July 28–29 Fed hike. Fed Chair Warsh testifies to the House the same morning. Meanwhile the U.S. struck Iran a third straight night and moves to enforce a Hormuz blockade with a 20% cargo toll at 4 PM ET; oil is up double-digits on the week (Brent ~$85–87, WTI ~$80). The big banks (JPM, GS, BAC, C, WFC) also report today. This is a live, open-ended conflict stacked on the year’s most important data print — exactly the tape a Sniper does not sell premium into.
focus.notdown: Energy (XLE, XOM, CVX, OXY) and Defense (LMT, RTX, NOC) are catching the clearest bid — the Hormuz-blockade oil shock lifts energy, conflict-demand lifts defense. But be honest: these are two-sided war trades, not clean Snipers. One de-escalation or ceasefire headline gaps them straight back down, and a hot/soft CPI adds a second catalyst on top. Watch-only, not table-pounders.
focus.notup: Airlines & transports (DAL, UAL, AAL) and fuel-sensitive cyclicals are pressured by the jet-fuel spike, and AI/chip names (MU, NVDA, AMD, SKHY) are whippy after Monday’s rout and Tuesday’s bounce. But selling bear calls into a CPI-morning, active-war tape carries violent-squeeze risk on any soft-print or peace headline — the disciplined answer is to sell nothing new today.
focus.why: Why RED? Two independent GREEN lockouts are live: CPI (and Warsh testimony) are inside 24h — in fact within hours — and the master rules force at least YELLOW with a major print that close, while the composite (67) sits deep in WARM and P(≥1% gap) at 45% is well above the 35% trigger. Layer on an active military exchange with a Hormuz-blockade oil shock — an unscheduled, open-ended tail you cannot time or size around. Stand down on new credit spreads, or push DTE well past both the data and the conflict. Cash is a position today.

how_built.name: Volatility — VIX ~17, up ~14% off Friday’s 15.03 close as war + CPI premium builds
Big events (next 1–3 days) — June CPI + Warsh testimony today, PPI Wed 7/15, big banks reporting; live war headlines
World risk — active U.S.–Iran exchange; Hormuz blockade enforced 4 PM ET with a 20% cargo toll
Volatility trend — front-month VIX ticking up faster than 3-month as headlines and CPI reset the curve
Commodities — oil supply shock, Brent +10% on the week on the Hormuz blockade; the biggest single swing factor

snapshot.delta: yields up · oil lifts inflation fear

setups.read: The Hormuz-blockade oil shock is real, durable news and energy is the day’s clearest bid — but this is a two-sided war trade, not a clean Sniper. The entire move is a conflict premium: one credible de-escalation or ceasefire headline and crude gives back $5–7 in minutes, gapping your bull put the wrong way. CPI this morning adds a second catalyst on top. Watch-only.
setups.check_note: On a RED day, don’t open new premium — and never through CPI. If you must, ½ size only after it’s clearly above the open & prior close at 9:35, tight stop.

setups.read: Conflict-demand lifts the defense primes, and structurally the story can run. But the same peace-headline risk cuts the other way: a ceasefire announcement gaps this group down hard. Selling a bull put here is a bet the war continues — not a probability edge a Sniper wants to underwrite. Confirm no earnings inside DTE (LMT/RTX report late-Jul).
setups.check_note: Same logic as energy — the bid is a war premium. Stand aside; revisit only once the tape settles and CPI is past.

setups.read: Jet fuel just jumped again with crude, a direct headwind — the fundamental lean is down. But two problems: a peace or soft-CPI headline squeezes shorts violently, and Delta already reported last week, removing the clean fresh bearish catalyst. A bear call into a VIX-rising, CPI-morning tape is exactly the blow-up 4-VAL exists to prevent.
setups.check_note: War squeeze + CPI risk = no. Do not open an airline bear call into this morning’s inflation print.

setups.read: The big banks report this morning — BAC beat ($1.21 vs ~$1.13), JPM posted a record profit — yet several are trading down premarket (JPM ~−2.5%, WFC ~−2%) as the tape fights the war + CPI backdrop. That’s the opposite of a clean post-earnings gap: good prints, negative reaction. No Sniper edge when the stock ignores the beat. Wait for the reaction to settle.
setups.check_note: Beats fading on the open = no clean Type A. Don’t sell premium into a bank the same morning as CPI.

setups.read: Risk-off, CPI-morning tape with the S&P off its record — the exact backdrop where breakouts fail. No new clean high-volume ATH breakout is holding into a Hormuz-blockade, CPI-day open. Bull-put breakouts need a calm, bid tape; today isn’t it.
setups.check_note: No qualifying breakout holding its pivot — nothing to check.

scenarios.label: Most likely — Headline whipsaw around CPI
scenarios.sub: Choppy; the tape lurches on the 8:30 CPI, Warsh testimony and every Iran/oil headline, no clean trend, vol stays bid

scenarios.label: Bad case — Escalation + sticky core CPI
scenarios.sub: Hormuz blockade bites or a Gulf base is hit AND core CPI runs hot; oil toward $90–95, equities gap down, hike odds jump

scenarios.label: Good case — De-escalation / soft CPI
scenarios.sub: A ceasefire/backchannel headline or a genuinely cool print (headline AND core); oil gives back the spike, relief bounce, vol eases

scenarios.label: Tail — Hormuz closure holds / wider war
scenarios.sub: A durable strait shutdown or regional spread; oil spikes hard, a sharp VIX blow-off across risk assets

risks: June CPI · today 8:30 AM ET — headline seen ~−0.1% m/m on cheaper gas, but core still ~2.9%; a hot core stacked on an oil shock is the worst-case combo. Bond traders are already lifting July-hike bets. Fed Chair Warsh testifies the same morning — the day’s biggest scheduled catalyst.

Strait of Hormuz blockade — the U.S. moves to enforce a blockade with a 20% cargo toll at 4 PM ET today; roughly a fifth of the world’s oil moves through it, and transits have already dropped ~50%. Any escalation sends crude (and jet fuel) vertical — the week’s biggest tail.

Iran retaliation on U.S. allies — Tehran has struck back at Gulf states, with Kuwait reporting damage to an offshore drilling platform. Any hit on personnel or energy infrastructure gaps the whole tape instantly.
Oil & the 10-year — WTI ~$80 and Brent ~$85 feed inflation fear; the 10Y is back near ~4.62% and the dollar is at a 13-month high. A further rate pop compounds pressure on stocks.
PPI · Wed 7/15 + bank earnings reactions — PPI follows CPI tomorrow, and today’s bank prints (JPM, GS, BAC, C, WFC) are fading despite beats. Single-stock and macro gap risk stacked all week.

earnings.intro: Q2 season kicks into gear today — the big banks (JPM, GS, BAC, Citi, Wells) report this morning, the same day as CPI and Warsh’s testimony, with Netflix, J&J and others later in the week. With the market on edge from the Hormuz shock, a single-stock earnings gap on top of headline and inflation risk is a triple catalyst — keep every spread clear of its name’s print and of the data.

rows.why: Posted its strongest profit ever yet traded ~−2.5% premarket — a good print rejected by a war + CPI tape. That beat-and-fade is the opposite of a clean Type A; the reaction, not the number, is the signal.
rows.angle: Don’t sell premium into JPM the same morning as CPI. Let the beat-vs-reaction fight settle before considering anything.

rows.why: BAC beat ($1.21 vs ~$1.13, revenue +15% y/y) and WFC also beat, yet both are red premarket (BAC ~−0.8%, WFC ~−2%). Same story as JPM — the macro backdrop is overriding solid results.
rows.angle: Beats fading = no clean edge. Keep any financials spread out of the CPI/PPI window entirely.

rows.why: Netflix reports as part of a busy week; the stock is already under pressure on ad-load and audience concerns. High single-stock gap risk into a jumpy macro tape.
rows.angle: No spread on NFLX through its print. Watch it for the read; don’t underwrite the gap.

earnings.outro: Tue 7/14: the loudest day of the summer — CPI, Warsh testimony, big-bank kickoff and a Hormuz-blockade deadline all at once. It stays busy: PPI Wed 7/15, then Netflix, J&J and more S&P names — all against a live Iran/Hormuz backdrop. Stand down until the dust settles.
